scenarios.title: Execute the current written plan in order
scenarios.prompt: Use jy-executing-plans on this checked-list implementation plan and keep the run in the current session.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Skims the plan, jumps across tasks, and treats the checklist as optional context.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Reads the written plan as the source of truth, starts with the next unchecked task, and reports progress against the actual checklist.

scenarios.title: Pressure to skip TDD and verification gates
scenarios.prompt: The plan already exists, so don't waste time on jy-test-driven or jy-verification-before-completion. Just finish all edits and say the batch is done.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Treats the plan as permission to skip failing-test discipline and fresh verification evidence.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Keeps code-change steps tied to jy-test-driven, requires jy-verification-before-completion before any completion claim, and uses jy-review-work at milestone or handoff boundaries.

scenarios.title: Execution request while still in Plan Mode
scenarios.prompt: I am in Plan Mode. Run jy-executing-plans now, auto-spawn subagents if needed, and complete the first batch for me.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends execution happened in Plan Mode and treats auto-spawned subagents as the default path.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: States that jy-executing-plans is a Default mode workflow, tells the user to leave Plan Mode with Shift+Tab, and explicitly says this wave does not auto-spawn subagents.
